summary: ResNet-18 trained to 95.50% on CIFAR-10 (seed=42, 200 epochs, 35.5 min on RTX 4090). Full-model Hessian computation verified via dattri IFAttributorEKFAC (10 test x 100 train, 8.2s, 10.43GB peak). TRAK attribution also verified. Environment fully set up.
key_findings: ResNet-18 reaches 95.50% test acc, well above 93% threshold
dattri IFAttributorEKFAC works for full-model Conv2d (pyDVL does NOT support Conv2d)
Full-model per-sample gradients use ~10GB for 100 train samples at batch=4
TRAK (traker package) works correctly; import as 'from trak import TRAKer'
RepSim via avgpool cosine similarity works as expected
CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER=PCI_BUS_ID required to match nvidia-smi GPU numbering
risks_identified: Memory constraint: Full 50K train x 500 test attribution will require chunked computation
GPU 3 may share memory with other users' processes (~7GB occupied during testing)
pyDVL cannot be used for ResNet-18 (Conv2d limitation); must use dattri exclusively
fast_jl not installed for TRAK CudaProjector; using BasicProjector (slower but functional)
recommendations_for_next_phase: Use dattri IFAttributorEKFAC for Phase 1 attribution computation (not pyDVL)
Use batch_size=4-8 for per-sample gradient computation to stay within 24GB
Process attributions in chunks: 50 test points at a time against training data
Install fast_jl for TRAK CudaProjector speedup if TRAK-50 computation is slow
Consider installing flash-attention for training speedup if multi-seed training is bottleneck
